claim: AI agents will become the primary economic actors in the future, eventually being the only entities humans trust with significant assets due to superior decision-making and reliability.
actionable: When building financial or economic systems, design them with the assumption that AI agents will eventually be major participants and plan accordingly for agent-native features.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: KYA (Know Your Agent) frameworks are needed to identify and authenticate agent identities with the same rigor as KYC for individuals, enabling trust in agent-to-agent transactions.
actionable: Develop cryptographic identity verification for agents that works analogously to human KYC, allowing you to verify which organization owns an agent before interaction.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: Risk infrastructure must shift from blocking all bots to identifying good actors and applying policies (spending limits, decision auditing) rather than assuming bot = bad actor.
actionable: Replace blanket bot-blocking rules with risk frameworks that enable good agents while maintaining policy enforcement (spending caps, audit trails, permission scoping).
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: Current financial infrastructure and LLM reliability mean agent-to-agent payments and agent autonomy remain unrealistic, though LLM quality is sufficient for supervised decision-making.
actionable: Keep humans in the loop for financial decisions until both agent reliability and infrastructure mature; focus on agent-assisted (not autonomous) financial workflows.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: Agent-to-agent communication standards (like SSL/TLS for HTTP) are missing; the field is fragmented with competing protocols (X42, MCP) with no clear winner or broad adoption.
actionable: Monitor standardization efforts around agent communication but expect continued fragmentation for 1-2 years; design systems to adapt to multiple communication protocols.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: AI agents will need banking services: payments, foreign exchange, lending, credit access, and interest-bearing accounts—requiring new AI-native bank infrastructure.
actionable: If building agent platforms at scale, anticipate the need for agent-native financial services and design for programmatic transactions and account management.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)

claim: Building foundational infrastructure requires balancing engineering, product, sales/partnerships, AND regulatory stakeholders as co-equal forces, not supporting roles.
actionable: In regulated domains, bring regulatory representatives into product planning early, treating compliance as a requirement generator rather than a gate.
source_title: When Bots Have Bank Accounts: The Rise of the Agent Economy (With Sean Neville, Catena Labs)
